items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary) **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit 48e9f7bd852c033dbe6680c68a3197cb55722eca (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs: 1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call. ## Gaps Found - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says "Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues" — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded. - The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour. - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them. - The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback).
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description: ```markdown ### Logging `AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category: | Event | Level | Fields | |-------|-------|--------| | Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` | | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` | | Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` | > **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data. To enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. ``` **Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry: ```markdown ### Inbound request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs. ``` #### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`. Key log events per endpoint: - **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model. - **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count. - **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count. - **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status. - **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag. - **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID. `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Enable these logs in `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. ``` **Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry: ```markdown ### Session ID (`agent_session_id`) Each response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order: 1. Explicit value from the request payload. 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable. 3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`. 4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available. This matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)
